results.description: Suggest Builder pattern for complex structs (advisory)

violations.message: Struct 'StageFailed' has 5 fields (0 optional) - consider Builder pattern

results.description: api/ layer must not contain `pub struct` outside designated struct homes — flat layout: api/error/, api/types/; domain-grouped layout: api/<domain>/errors/, api/<domain>/types/

violations.message: 'ClosedEventSource' at line 8 — pub struct in api/main\src\api\event\closed_event_source.rs is outside designated struct homes (api/types/ or api/{theme}/types/)
violations.fix_hint: Move the struct to core/ and expose it behind an api/ trait; to api/error/ or api/<domain>/errors/ if it is an error type; or to api/types/ or api/<domain>/types/ if it is a value object

violations.message: 'InProcessHandlerRegistry' at line 8 — pub struct in api/main\src\api\handler\in_process_handler_registry.rs is outside designated struct homes (api/types/ or api/{theme}/types/)
violations.fix_hint: Move the struct to core/ and expose it behind an api/ trait; to api/error/ or api/<domain>/errors/ if it is an error type; or to api/types/ or api/<domain>/types/ if it is a value object

results.description: api/ must contain error types — either a flat api/error/ subdirectory or domain-grouped api/<domain>/errors/ directories; at least one must exist
api/ root must not contain loose .rs files or mixed-layout directories — only mod.rs at root; no flat traits/types/error/ dirs when domain-grouped layout (api/<domain>/traits/) is active

violations.message: Mixed layout: flat 'error/' at api/ root conflicts with domain-grouped api/<domain>/error/ directories — use one layout consistently
violations.fix_hint: Move the contents of api/error/ into the appropriate api/<domain>/error/ directory and remove the flat error/ dir

results.description: Source files must not suppress the dead_code lint via #[allow(dead_code)]

violations.message: #[allow(dead_code)] at line 4 — remove the suppression and delete or wire up the dead code
violations.expected: remove #[allow(dead_code)] and delete or implement the dead item
violations.fix_hint: Fix the issue detected by builtin check 'no_allow_dead_code'
